title: Something Massive Is Pulling Our Entire Galaxy Toward It
hook: Our galaxy, along with thousands of others, is being pulled through space at over a million miles an hour toward something we can't directly see.
body: Astronomers call it the Great Attractor, and the reason it's hard to see is almost funny: it sits directly behind the crowded, dusty center of our own galaxy, in what's known as the zone of avoidance. What we can measure is its gravitational pull on entire clusters of galaxies across a region hundreds of millions of light years wide. Using X-ray and radio telescopes that can see through the dust, researchers traced the pull to a dense collection of galaxy clusters, but debate continues over whether that fully accounts for the strength of the pull, or whether it's one waypoint toward an even larger structure further out.
cta: An entire section of the universe is hidden just because of where we happen to be standing in it. Follow for more scale-of-the-universe mysteries.
description: What is the Great Attractor, and why is the region of space pulling our galaxy toward it still partly hidden from view?
